user_query:
Please set up a local markdown project tracker using the data in input/projects.json and the config in input/config.yaml.

Requirements:
1) Create individual project files under output/projects/, named by each project's slug field with .md (e.g., output/projects/website-redesign.md). For each project file include:
   - Title: "# Project: <Name>"
   - Status line using these exact emojis and labels: 🟢 Active | 🟡 On Hold | 🔴 Blocked | ✅ Done
   - Priority (P1/P2/P3)
   - Started and Target dates
   - One-liner
   - "Priority Score: <weighted score rounded to 2 decimals>" where the weights come from input/config.yaml (revenue 30%, time_to_completion 20%, strategic_alignment 25%, energy 15%, dependencies 10%)
   - "## Milestones" with the checklist rendered from the JSON
   - "## Progress Log" with dated entries from the JSON
   - "## Decisions" table (create the header even if empty)
   - "## Blockers" section (list unresolved blockers)
   - If the project is detected as stalled (see #3), include a visible note near the top like "⚠️ Stalled"

2) Create a central dashboard at output/projects/DASHBOARD.md with:
   - "Last updated: <current_date from config>"
   - A table header: "| Project | Status | Priority | Next Action | Due |"
   - Three sections: "## Active Projects", "## On Hold", and "## Completed"
   - List all projects under the appropriate section, with rows containing Project name, Status (with emoji), Priority, Next Action (use the first incomplete milestone title), and Due (target date or "-" if none)
   - For any stalled project, append "⚠️ Stalled" in the same line as the project entry

3) Stalled detection rules (use thresholds from config):
   - Stalled if no progress log entry in >= stale_threshold_days
   - OR has any unresolved blocker older than >= blocker_stale_days
   - OR status is Active with no recent milestone movement (you may treat this the same as no progress for this task)
   Evaluate staleness relative to current_date from config.

4) Generate a weekly review file at output/projects/weekly/weekly_review_2026-04-13_to_2026-04-19.md with:
   - "# Weekly Project Review — 2026-04-13 to 2026-04-19"
   - Summary lines for counts: Active, Completed this week, Stalled, New
   - "Completed this week" should list projects with status ✅ Done and a last progress date within the given week range (inclusive)
   - A "Per-Project Status" section with brief notes
   - A "Recommendations" section that includes pause/kill suggestions for any Active or On Hold projects with weighted priority score < 2.5

Data and config locations:
- Read input/projects.json for all project records (including slugs, milestones, progress logs, blockers, and priority factor scores on a 1–5 scale)
- Read input/config.yaml for current_date, weekly review date range, stale thresholds, and priority weights

Deliverables to write:
- output/projects/DASHBOARD.md
- output/projects/<slug>.md for every project in the input
- output/projects/weekly/weekly_review_2026-04-13_to_2026-04-19.md

Be careful with:
- Using the exact status emojis as shown
- Rounding the Priority Score to two decimals
- Using the slug from the input for file names
- Next Action = first incomplete milestone title
- Including "⚠️ Stalled" both in the project file and in the corresponding dashboard line for stalled projects
